objective: Architectural sign-off on grove init --as mcp-llm: Target::McpLlm variant, explore-mode .mcp.json, idempotent CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md steering, non-TTY guard, dry-run, and first-run TUI delegation.
key_changes: Clean additive CLI change — Target::McpLlm auto-derives --as mcp-llm and --help via clap ValueEnum; no bespoke arg plumbing.
Reuses proven idioms: .mcp.json read/merge pattern, grove:start/end sentinel idempotency, and the T05 IsTerminal guard.
Existing init targets left byte-untouched via a write_harness McpLlm early-return; all 19 pre-existing unit tests remain green.
writes_mcp() stays false for McpLlm so the generic ready block never double-prints.
serve --explore registered per the stdio MCP server contract; no protocol surface change.
No migrations, schema, or deployment-topology impact; single-binary distribution unaffected.
Backward-compatible: existing mcp|skill|both flows unchanged; docs (README.md, docs/setup.md) updated.
Code review and validation both approved: 200 tests pass, clippy -D warnings clean, no stray fastcontext string.
Follow-up: cosmetic rename of shared CLAUDE_START/END constants to neutral GROVE_START/END now that they span two harness files.
Follow-up: reconcile AC2(b) wording (says pre-populated) with implemented skip-TUI-on-rerun behaviour if the AC is revisited.
